_comment: This is a sample showing the JSON schema for build_report.js. Real Claude usage would produce findings by combining the mechanical output of analyze.py with judgment-based findings from reading the manuscript. Remove the _comment fields when using as a template.
manuscript_title: Full title of the manuscript as it appears in the file

executive_summary._comment: Three short paragraphs summarising the state of the manuscript.
executive_summary.good_state: Paragraph describing what's in good shape, with specific metrics (title length, word count, etc.).
executive_summary.blockers: Paragraph introducing the critical issues that need to be fixed before submission.
blocker_list: Short description of first critical issue
Short description of second critical issue
Short description of third critical issue
executive_summary.revision_note: Optional paragraph used only for revision reviews summarising what was fixed since the previous review.

fixed_since_previous: Only used for revision reviews. Each string is one bullet describing a resolved issue from the previous review.

findings.title: Short title visible at the top of the detailed entry (1 line)
findings.summary: One-line version for the at-a-glance table — should be different from title; describe the symptom
findings.location: Section reference using § for subsections (e.g., 'STAR Methods § LLM inference')
findings.issue: Plain text describing the problem in detail. Include a verbatim quote of the problematic text when it is short enough to fit. Explain WHY this is an issue if not obvious.
findings.action: Concrete edit the user should make. Should be specific enough to apply directly, not a vague suggestion. If verification is needed, say so.

findings.title: Another finding
findings.location: Where it is in the manuscript

separate_uploads.title: Highlights and eTOC blurb (single Word file)
separate_uploads.description: Specific guidance for this manuscript on what to prepare.
